body.title: [sprint] Cost-ordered stuck-ladder controller + rung-choice log (builder starve-curve)
body.description:
**Problem:** once escalation is off, a stuck DS has no structured exit — it just hits the turn cap. We need the cost-ordered ladder from the design: on stuck, pick the cheapest viable rung and RECORD which rung was taken. The distribution over rungs IS the builder starve-curve.

**Proposed shape:** a stuck-ladder controller invoked when a harvest_mode run reaches a wall. Four cost-ordered rungs: (1) ANSWER — consult already-compiled artifacts (build-packet / prereg / corpus) for a known answer [free, the win]; (2) DROP-TICKET — file the defeating question, mark the current ticket needs-design, move on [cheap, async] (the drop-ticket BODY is the next ticket; here just the rung + call-out point); (3) HALT — the built-but-unused HALT primitive [blocks]; (4) CALL-CC — sync escalation to CC [expensive; its FREQUENCY is the starved-resource metric]. Emit ONE structured rung-choice record per stuck event: {ticket_id, rung, tier, turn_reached, reason}. This ticket wires the controller + rungs 1/3/4 + the log; rung-2's classifier+drop lives in the next ticket (this one calls a stub drop hook).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/agentic_loop.py or a new stuck_ladder.py under devices/inference/ (OOP: a StuckLadder class the loop calls), the HALT primitive wiring (unused today), a new starve-curve log/record sink (reuse io_corpus-style append or a dedicated rung log under ~/.unseen_university/logs/inference/).

**Design rules:** OOP-first (StuckLadder class, not loose functions); log every rung decision (state change + interface crossing); flat-file or Postgres for the rung record, never SQLite; docs-live-in-code on the ladder module.

**Scope boundary:** the controller + rungs 1/3/4 + the rung-choice record. NOT the design-vs-capability classifier (next ticket) — rung 2 calls a stub here.

**Validation:** a harvest_mode stuck run emits exactly one rung-choice record naming the rung; a run that hits call-CC increments a countable call-CC frequency.

**Observable via:** log.info('StuckLadder: ticket=%s rung=%s tier=%s turn=%d') per stuck event.

**Test plan:** hermetic — drive the ladder with a synthetic stuck event; assert one rung-choice record with the expected rung + fields. PROOF NODE: a stuck event with no compiled answer and HALT-unavailable routes to call-CC AND emits a rung-choice record — red (today: no ladder, no record) → green. A naive ladder that logs nothing / picks the wrong rung fails the assert.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
